subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Why Rocky Planets Differ
description: Learn Why Rocky Planets Differ in one minute.

hook.text: Mercury, Venus, Earth, and Mars formed from the same dust cloud. Why are they so different today?

buildup.text: Distance from the Sun set starting temperatures. Closer planets got more heat, changing what could form.

discovery.text: Size matters most. Larger worlds hold thicker atmospheres, which trap heat and enable liquid water.

twist.text: Venus and Earth started nearly identical. A runaway greenhouse on Venus shows how fragile the balance is.

climax.text: Mars was too small to keep its air. Mercury too close and tiny. Only Earth hit the sweet spot.

punchline.text: Same dust, different distances. Four worlds, four fates.

quiz.question: What is the most important factor that made the rocky planets so different?
options: Their size and distance from the Sun
The order in which they formed
The number of moons each planet captured
